issue.author: dtcxzyw
issue.body:
Reproducer: https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/jHhApt

```llvm
; bin/opt -passes=indvars test.ll -S
define i16 @src() {
entry:
  %xor = xor i32 1, 0
  %cmp4 = icmp samesign ult i32 %xor, 155
  br i1 %cmp4, label %for.body10, label %if.end

for.body10:                                       ; preds = %for.body10, %entry
  %f.addr.0 = phi i8 [ 1, %for.body10 ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %cmp8.old = icmp slt i8 %f.addr.0, 1
  br i1 %cmp8.old, label %for.body10, label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %for.body10, %entry
  ret i16 0
}
```

```llvm
define i16 @tgt() {
entry:
  %xor = xor i32 1, 0
  %cmp4 = icmp samesign ult i32 %xor, 155
  br i1 %cmp4, label %for.body10.preheader, label %if.end

for.body10.preheader:                             ; preds = %entry
  br label %for.body10

for.body10:                                       ; preds = %for.body10.preheader, %for.body10
  br i1 true, label %for.body10, label %if.end.loopexit

if.end.loopexit:                                  ; preds = %for.body10
  br label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %if.end.loopexit, %entry
  ret i16 0
}
```
After indvars, the program runs into an infinite loop (`for.body10 -> for.body10`).
This is a recent bug.
LLVM version: 59cbe2ff591d91e8375cfb4f4ba59dff49a82f4f

cc @artagnon

comments.author: artagnon
comments.body:
I'm slightly confused by this.

The following seems unchanged, and evaluates to true (I wonder why the xor isn't folded?):

```llvm
  %xor = xor i32 1, 0
  %cmp4 = icmp samesign ult i32 %xor, 155
```

How did this get folded to true? It's signed-less-than, which means the backedge should be taken once.

```llvm
  %f.addr.0 = phi i8 [ 1, %for.body10 ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %cmp8.old = icmp slt i8 %f.addr.0, 1
```

Not sure if this has something to do with samesign.

comments.author: dtcxzyw
artagnon
comments.body:
> Ping. Any progress?

Since the patch has been reverted, what's pending is a re-land, but it's not very high priority for me at the moment: feel free to work on it if you like.

issue.title: SCEV: buggy samesign handling in #124270

tests.test_body:
define i32 @exit_count_samesign(i32 %iter.count, ptr %ptr) {
entry:
  br label %outer.loop

ph:                                               ; preds = %inner.loop, %outer.loop
  br label %outer.loop

outer.loop:                                       ; preds = %ph, %entry
  %iv.outer = phi i32 [ %iv.outer.1, %ph ], [ %iter.count, %entry ]
  %iv.outer.1 = add nsw i32 %iv.outer, -1
  %ext.outer = zext nneg i32 %iv.outer.1 to i64
  %gep.outer = getelementptr double, ptr %ptr, i64 %ext.outer
  store double poison, ptr %gep.outer, align 8
  %exit.cond.outer = icmp samesign ugt i32 %iv.outer, 1
  br i1 %exit.cond.outer, label %inner.loop, label %ph

inner.loop:                                       ; preds = %inner.loop, %outer.loop
  %iv.inner = phi i32 [ %iv.next, %inner.loop ], [ 0, %outer.loop ]
  %ext.inner = zext nneg i32 %iv.inner to i64
  %gep.inner = getelementptr double, ptr %ptr, i64 %ext.inner
  store double poison, ptr %gep.inner, align 8
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv.inner, 1
  %exit.cond.inner = icmp slt i32 %iv.next, %iv.outer.1
  br i1 %exit.cond.inner, label %inner.loop, label %ph

exit:                                             ; No predecessors!
  ret i32 0
}

define void @implied1_neg(i32 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp1 = icmp sgt i32 %n, 0
  %n.div.2 = sdiv i32 %n, 2
  call void (i1, ...) @llvm.experimental.guard(i1 %cmp1) [ "deopt"() ]
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %header, %entry
  %indvar = phi i32 [ %indvar.next, %header ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %indvar.next = add i32 %indvar, 1
  %exitcond = icmp sgt i32 %n.div.2, %indvar.next
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %header
  ret void
}

declare void @llvm.experimental.guard(i1, ...)

define void @implied3_samesign(i32 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp1 = icmp samesign ugt i32 %n, -2
  %n.div.2 = sdiv i32 %n, 2
  call void @llvm.assume(i1 %cmp1)
  br label %header

header:                                           ; preds = %header, %entry
  %indvar = phi i32 [ %indvar.next, %header ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %indvar.next = add i32 %indvar, 1
  %exitcond = icmp sge i32 %n.div.2, %indvar
  br i1 %exitcond, label %header, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %header
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @iv_zext_zext_gt_slt(i32 %iter.count, ptr %ptr) {
entry:
  br label %outer.loop

ph:                                               ; preds = %inner.loop, %outer.loop
  br label %outer.loop

outer.loop:                                       ; preds = %ph, %entry
  %iv.outer = phi i32 [ %iv.outer.1, %ph ], [ %iter.count, %entry ]
  %iv.outer.1 = add nsw i32 %iv.outer, -1
  %ext.outer = zext nneg i32 %iv.outer.1 to i64
  %gep.outer = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %ext.outer
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.outer, align 1
  %exit.cond.outer = icmp samesign ugt i32 %iv.outer, 1
  br i1 %exit.cond.outer, label %inner.loop, label %ph

inner.loop:                                       ; preds = %inner.loop, %outer.loop
  %iv.inner = phi i32 [ %iv.next, %inner.loop ], [ 0, %outer.loop ]
  %ext.inner = zext nneg i32 %iv.inner to i64
  %gep.inner = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %ext.inner
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.inner, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv.inner, 1
  %exit.cond.inner = icmp slt i32 %iv.next, %iv.outer.1
  br i1 %exit.cond.inner, label %inner.loop, label %ph

exit:                                             ; No predecessors!
  ret i32 0
}

target datalayout = "e-m:e-p270:32:32-p271:32:32-p272:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-f80:128-n8:16:32:64-S128"

define i32 @iv_sext_sext_gt_slt(i32 %iter.count, ptr %ptr) {
entry:
  br label %outer.loop

ph:                                               ; preds = %inner.loop, %outer.loop
  br label %outer.loop

outer.loop:                                       ; preds = %ph, %entry
  %iv.outer = phi i32 [ %iv.outer.1, %ph ], [ %iter.count, %entry ]
  %iv.outer.1 = add nsw i32 %iv.outer, -1
  %ext.outer = sext i32 %iv.outer.1 to i64
  %gep.outer = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %ext.outer
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.outer, align 1
  %exit.cond.outer = icmp samesign ugt i32 %iv.outer, 1
  br i1 %exit.cond.outer, label %inner.loop, label %ph

inner.loop:                                       ; preds = %inner.loop, %outer.loop
  %iv.inner = phi i32 [ %iv.next, %inner.loop ], [ 0, %outer.loop ]
  %ext.inner = sext i32 %iv.inner to i64
  %gep.inner = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr, i64 %ext.inner
  store i8 0, ptr %gep.inner, align 1
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i32 %iv.inner, 1
  %exit.cond.inner = icmp slt i32 %iv.next, %iv.outer.1
  br i1 %exit.cond.inner, label %inner.loop, label %ph

exit:                                             ; No predecessors!
  ret i32 0
}

define void @pr126409() {
entry:
  %x = xor i32 0, 1
  %cmp = icmp samesign ult i32 %x, 155
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %if.end

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %phi = phi i8 [ 1, %for.body ], [ 0, %entry ]
  %cmp2 = icmp slt i8 %phi, 1
  br i1 %cmp2, label %for.body, label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  ret void
}
